text:
User: deeply review this repo -- I want you to help me with marketing, copywriting, launch strategy, specially positioning -- start with a swarm of sonnet agents to help you gather data, both from the code and from docs (no docs is to be taken too seriously -- might have hallucinations), as well as online market research -- once you've done a first exploration use a fable agent to get high-level ideas, direction, concepts. The first target is to rewrite from scratch the README.md and land high-level copywriting for the landing page, anchored in uniqueness, USP and any best copywriting practices. [...] Lead with the grounded floor: "Citizenship for your agents" + host-neutral durable identity + cross-host/cross-device awareness for your own fleet (Product A, Rung 0–1) — this is real, tested, and matches tenet #9 (no dashboard) and #3 (single-player value day one). Use the vision/society material (multi-app agents, coordination, cross-person) as clearly-labeled "where this is headed," not as present-tense claims — the docs' own authors were careful to mark that boundary and copy should preserve it. [...] - **The shipped reality is more concrete than I expected.** 374 passing tests; the standout *working* mechanism is session-targeted `@mentions` that get **injected as real pasted turns into another agent's live terminal pane, host-neutrally** (Claude Code ↔ Codex ↔ opencode). That's a demonstrable "so what" — and crucially it's the *awareness/messaging floor*, not the coordination/locking ceiling the doctrine warns against.
- **Honesty guardrails for the copy** (from docs-vision): the beachhead persona is officially an *open question*; the multi-app "society" (todo agent, generative podcast agent) and all cross-person features are *aspirational/unbuilt*; the whole product-spec is draft. The README must lead present-tense with the grounded floor and clearly label the vision as horizon.

This tightens my earlier read: anchor on **"citizenship for your agents"** — durable identity + shared awareness for the agents you already run — with the live cross-host `@mention` injection as the concrete hero proof. [...] Dense brief below. Source hierarchy: docs/product-spec/ (13 chapters, all "Status: draft, 2026-06-08" — explicitly working notes/positions-to-argue-with, NOT shipped-feature claims) is the deepest doctrine. README.md and AGENTS.md describe what's actually implemented (much narrower, verified). Wiki guides/nouns are LLM-compiled "warm/medium confidence" distillations — treat as secondary paraphrase, not source. [...] **Explicitly self-flagged as draft/unproven/aspirational by the docs themselves (not me inferring — they say so):**
- The ENTIRE product-spec/ directory is dated 2026-06-08, marked "draft," described as "Working notes... Everything here is a draft / a position to argue with." This is the authors being honest that vision.md and agent-society.md are not commitments. [...] - The whole todo-agent/podcast-agent multi-app society vignette (vision.md, agent-society.md) — presented as "a real, running example (not hypothetical)" but I could not find any corroborating evidence in README/AGENTS/architecture docs that a todo-app agent or podcast-generation capability actually exists and talks to tenex-edge today. The podcast-*player* agent's existence on relay.tenex.chat is separately claimed in ecosystem.md as real/shipping — but "podcast agent generates new episodes on demand" and "todo agent organizes your day" read as the aspirational extrapolation layered on top of a real shipping podcast *player*. Flag this distinction clearly in any copy: podcast-player-agent-exists ≠ generative-podcast-agent-exists.
